articles.title: GEN Z IS ALMOST 30: WHY MARKETERS NEED TO RETHINK EVERYTHING THEY KNOW ABOUT YOUTH CULTURE
articles.summary: Gen Z is almost 30 and leading culture, while Gen Alpha is already shaping trends and household spending. Both generations expect brands to go beyond surface-level purpose and empty trends. They demand real values built into everything from products to policies. They want brands that entertain and connect authentically, with content that ranges from quick, catchy moments to meaningful stories across different platforms. These groups also care deeply about social issues and sustainability, and they expect brands to show, not just say, their commitment. If a brand can't keep up with their evolving tastes and cultural influence, it risks being left behind.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY OFFERS MORE THAN TWICE ITS TOTAL VALUATION TO BUY CHROME FROM GOOGLE
articles.summary: Perplexity has made an unsolicited $34.5 billion all-cash offer to buy Chrome from Google, more than twice its $14 billion valuation. The bid is funded by venture investors and comes amid a possible court-ordered Chrome divestment.

articles.title: THE B2B MESSAGING PYRAMID
articles.summary: This 4-step model creates homepage copy that resonates by aligning with what buyers need to hear. It starts by understanding the buyer's context, including who they are and the situation they face, and then identifies the core friction that makes their current state unsustainable. Next, it presents a strategic unlock, bridging their pain to the transformation your solution enables, followed by proving why you are the best choice to deliver that shift. By moving from audience context to specific problems, then to the solution and your unique fit, this approach ensures messaging is relevant.

articles.title: WIN REPORT: HOW SIMPLIFYING A MODAL INCREASED TRANSACTIONS BY 13%
articles.summary: Simplifying a website's confirmation modal increased checkout page visits by 21% and overall transactions by 13% for a company selling sustainably harvested countertops. The original modal contained upsells, email capture, and multiple value propositions, which analytics showed caused a significant drop-off. The new version featured a larger product image, fewer details, no upsells, and two clear calls to action. The results showed that removing unnecessary elements reduced friction and improved conversions.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF APP STORE OPTIMIZATION (ASO): ADAPTING TO INTELLIGENT DISCOVERY
articles.summary: ASO is moving from static keyword playbooks to adaptive, AI-powered growth engines. Search results are now tailored to the individual, meaning the same query can result in different listings. Winning brands will personalize product pages, adopt AI-driven metadata updates, and optimize for voice and predictive discovery. Marketers should treat ASO like a performance channel, running rapid experiments and signaling app use cases clearly to surface at the right moments.

articles.title: HOW THREATSPIKE ADDED UP TO $75K IN PIPELINE PER CAMPAIGN WITH TLDR NEWSLETTER ADS
articles.summary: ThreatSpike ditched underperforming channels like Google and LinkedIn for TLDR. The results?

articles.title: 3 PROMPTS TO TRY ON GPT-5
articles.summary: These templated prompts perform real-time competitor analysis, discover recent high-engagement marketing tips from Reddit, and track the latest viral trends on TikTok, Instagram, X, and Reddit. They identify target industries, data collection methods, pricing visibility, key conversion pages, summarize practical marketing insights, and suggest brand tie-ins for trending content.

articles.title: HOW WE'RE USING AI IN NEW WAYS TO FIGHT INVALID TRAFFIC
articles.summary: Google is using LLMs to detect and block invalid traffic across its platforms. In partnership with Google Research and DeepMind, the Ad Traffic Quality team developed systems that analyze content, ad placements, and user interactions to identify deceptive practices, reducing invalid traffic by 40%. Automated and manual checks ensure advertisers are not charged for this activity.

articles.title: WHY REDDIT IS PIVOTING FROM SOCIAL PLATFORM TO ‘GO-TO SEARCH ENGINE'
articles.summary: Reddit is unifying its core search and AI-powered Reddit Answers, which has 6M weekly users, into a single experience alongside its 70 million weekly standard searches. The platform is looking to make search central to community navigation while capitalizing on its position as the 6th most searched term in Google in the US last year. Improved contextual ad relevance from conversation placements is also expected to extend into search advertising.

articles.title: OKTA'S NEWSROOM-DRIVEN CONTENT STRATEGY
articles.summary: Okta launched a six-person newsroom in April, moving editorial work from marketing to comms with a focus on second-day analysis that adds context after headlines break. C-suite videos replaced lengthy case studies to capture decision-making processes and bypass slow approvals. AI accelerates research, outlining, and personalization while humans handle the final third to preserve voice and judgment.

articles.title: THREADS TOPS 400M MONTHLY USERS
articles.summary: Threads now has 400M monthly active users and has grown daily mobile activity 127.8% year-over-year to 115.1M.

articles.summary: A roundup of “good” B2B marketing from Vector, Help Scout, and AirOps.

articles.title: HOW SWEET LOREN'S TURNED A HUGE SOCIAL MEDIA MISTAKE INTO A VIRAL WIN
articles.summary: Sweet Loren's accidental TikTok rename to “Ryan” was turned into a self-aware mini campaign that went viral.
